data.title: Australia's Woodside confronts protests, investor anger at annual meet
data.description:
Key points:
  • Activists disrupt annual meeting with whistles and heckling
  • Woodside forced to suspend proceedings, drown out noise with promotional videos
  • One-fifth of shareholders vote against climate committee chair

Climate change activists blew whistles and shouted on Thursday as they disrupted Australian gas producer Woodside Energy's annual general meeting, heckling Chief Executive Meg O’Neill and forcing several suspensions.

Investors also figured in the backlash to Woodside's gas projects and sustainability measures, similar to previous years, with Australian pension funds HESTA and Aware lodging protest votes against its director charged with climate risk oversight.

"I'd ask you to please be respectful of the other actual shareholders in the room who have a keen interest in understanding what we're doing to generate value for them," O'Neill told protesters who interrupted her opening address.

"You should be ashamed!" some of them had yelled.

The whistling and shouting began around 20 minutes into the meeting in the western Australian city of Perth, as O'Neill discussed Woodside’s gas portfolio, its contribution to society and role in meeting energy security and decarbonisation goals.

The behaviour was "unnecessary", Chairman Richard Goyder said.

Event organisers suspended proceedings and tried to drown out the noise by playing promotional videos about the company's energy projects and sponsorship of football club the Fremantle Dockers.

"We have plenty more of these videos we can play," O’Neill added.

O'Neill and Goyder also faced scrutiny from meeting attendees who questioned the company's carbon emissions and the impact of the proposed North West Shelf project extension on nearby Murujuga rock art and sea turtles in the Scott Reef.

A coalition of environmental groups - Go Beyond Gas, Greenpeace and the Conservation Council of WA - also protested outside.

Last year's proceedings featured similar backlash with shareholders rebuking Woodside's emissions plan and activists crashing the meeting due to concerns over the company's pursuit of new gas projects.

The company also greenlit a $17.5-billion liquefied natural gas project at Louisiana in the United States last week, which would take its total LNG output to 24 million tonnes per annum (Mtpa) in the next decade, or more than 5% of global supply.

Ahead of the annual meeting, influential proxy adviser Glass Lewis recommended that shareholders block the re-election of independent director Ann Pickard, who chairs the oversight committee on climate risk.

It cited an inadequate response to an "ongoing pattern of significant shareholder opposition to the company’s climate strategy".

The funds HESTA and Aware, as well as Norway’s Storebrand, opposed Pickard’s re-election, while U.S. pension funds CalPERS and CALSTRS voted against director Ben Wyatt.

"The steps taken by Woodside so far fall short of what is needed to position it for the global transition to a low-carbon future and the company needs to do more," HESTA said in a statement.

One-fifth (19.45%) of shareholders voted against Pickard's election, the worst vote on record against a Woodside committee chair, according to the Australasian Centre for Corporate Responsibility.

data.title: Italy's BPER posts 43.2% rise in first quarter net profit
data.description:
Italy's BPER Banca posted a 43.2% rise in first-quarter net profit on Thursday, driven by fee and trading income that more than countered a decline in net interest margin.

Net profit for the January-March period was 442.9 million euros ($500.74 million).

Italy's fourth-largest bank, which launched a 4.3 billion euro all-share bid for smaller rival Banca Popolare di Sondrio in February, saw revenues rise 5% in the first quarter.

Net fees rose 8.5%, while a narrowing spread between lending and deposit rates pushed net interest margin down 3.8%.

($1 = 0.8845 euros)

data.title: Life360 Bulls Paying Close Attention to 1Q User GrowthMarket Talk
data.description: Life360's bulls at Morgan Stanley will be paying close attention to the tracking app developer's active user numbers when it announces its 1Q results. They remind clients in a note that the final three months of 2024 showed the lowest number of new monthly active users in almost two years, with international markets especially soft. They say that user growth, engagement and retention are key to monetization, and are looking for 4.1 million net new users in the March quarter. MS has an overweight recommendation and a A$28.60 target price on the stock, which is up 0.6% at A$23.38. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch May 8
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Thursday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 2 hours):

INTEREST RATES

Poland's central bank cut interest rates for the first time since October 2023 on Wednesday, slashing the cost of credit by 50 basis points citing slowing inflation, weakening wage growth and worsening economic conditions.

On Thursday, the Governor of the National Bank of Poland (NBP) will hold a conference during which he will present the justification for the decision to cut interest rates.

POLAND AND GERMANY LEADERS MEETING

Germany's new chancellor Friedrich Merz signalled a new start in relations with European partners France and Poland, but there were indications of tough discussions ahead on thorny issues like illegal migration and defence financing.

MEETING OF EUROPEAN UNION FOREIGN MINISTERS

On Thursday, a two-day informal meeting of the foreign ministers of the European Union and the candidate and potential candidate countries to the EU will conclude in Warsaw.

POLISH-FRENCH TREATY

Poland is set to sign a treaty with France on Friday, which will serve as a basis for deepening cooperation in the arms and aviation sectors. The talks concern, among others, submarines, flying tankers and 155-millimeter ammunition, Rzeczpospolita daily reported.

PRESIDENTIAL ELECTIONS

The candidate of Poland's ruling Civic Coalition (KO), Warsaw Mayor Rafal Trzaskowski, leads ahead of a May 18 presidential election with support of 31%, according to an Opinia24 poll for TVN and TVN24 private broadcasters published on Wednesday.

BUDIMEX

Poland's biggest construction and engineering company Budimex reported a 4% drop in first-quarter net profit to 114.3 million zlotys ($30.3 million) on Thursday, hit by weather conditions that impacted sales of construction and assembly services in Central and Eastern Europe.

NETTO STORES

Netto, a Danish discount chain, aims to have 1,000 stores in Poland by the end of 2028. It will open some of them and intends to buy the rest, Puls Biznesu daily reported.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

Saudi Reinsurance Books Higher Q1 Net Profit, Insurance Revenue
data.description:
Saudi Reinsurance Co. (SASE:8200) said Thursday its net profit and insurance revenue for the first quarter jumped year over year.

Net profit after zakat attributable to shareholders for the three months ended March 31 was 35.4 million Saudi riyals, compared with 31.8 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.31 riyal from 0.36 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed insurer's insurance revenue was 323.4 million riyals, compared with 206.9 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Galaxy Entertainment's Revenue, Adjusted Profit Rose in First Quarter
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Galaxy Entertainment Group's revenue and adjusted profit rose in the first quarter as its gaming business continued to benefit from higher visitor arrivals to Macau.

Revenue rose to 11.20 billion Hong Kong dollars in the first quarter, equivalent to US$1.44 billion, compared with HK$10.55 billion the same period last year, the casino operator said Thursday. Adjusted Ebitda, a measure of core earnings that excludes interest, taxes, and other one-off costs, rose to HK$3.30 billion Hong Kong dollars, compared with HK$2.84 billion a year earlier.

"We were satisfied with our casinos' performance over the Chinese New Year and in particular the post Chinese New Year which experienced a longer tail than previously," Chairman Francis Lui Yiu Tung said.

The company acknowledged the recent comments by Macau's chief executive that the 2025 gross gaming revenue target may be challenging to achieve.

Galaxy Entertainment will continue to evaluate development opportunities in the Greater Bay Area in China, and overseas markets on a case-by-case basis, including Thailand, the company said.

Shares in Hong Kong were last 1.2% higher at HK$30.55.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Trump to Announce Trade Agreement With Britain
data.description:
By Natalie Andrews, Brian Schwartz and Alex Leary

WASHINGTON — President Trump is expected to announce a framework of a trade deal with the U.K. on Thursday, the first in what the White House hopes is a series of trade agreements since it imposed tariffs against allies and adversaries, according to people familiar with the negotiations.

The announcement, which Trump teased late Wednesday on Truth Social as being with a "big, and highly respected country," is expected to be a framework of an agreement with tariff adjustments.

"This announcement is likely just an agreement to start the negotiations, identifying a framework of issues to be discussed in the coming months," said international trade attorney Tim Brightbill. "We suspect that tariff rates, non-tariff barriers, and digital trade are all on the list — and there are difficult issues to address on all of these."

The White House and the British Embassy in Washington didn't respond to a request for comment.

The trade deal with the U.K. would be the first for Trump in his second term. Administration officials have said they are also in close talks with India and Japan, and the president is readying to go to the Middle East next week.

Last month, the U.S. imposed a new 10% tariff on most goods imported from the U.K, as part of the baseline tariff it imposed on all nations, and Britain is also subject to the 25% tariffs Trump imposed on all steel and aluminum imports on March 12.

The U.K. is hoping to get a reduction on the 25% tariffs the U.S. is levying on steel and automobiles, however the baseline 10% tariff will likely remain in place. In return, Britain is offering concessions on a digital tax it levies on big U.S. tech companies.

The U.K. and India announced Tuesday the completion of a trade agreement that had been stuck for several years.

Trump has paused sweeping tariffs on dozens of countries to allow for negotiations but has kept the 10% baseline tariff in place, as well as severe levies on Chinese imports, which have already led to some consumer-price increases on a variety of goods, including baby strollers, cribs and toys. The president has played down price concerns, saying last week that "maybe the children will have two dolls instead of 30." At the same time, Trump's unpredictable trade announcements have made investors nervous and pushed major stock indexes lower.

Trump is seeking an array of benefits for the U.S., including agreements that trading partners purchase more U.S. goods and curb non-tariff barriers. Trump argues that tariffs will encourage more manufacturing in the U.S.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has said that his priorities include striking trade deals with countries in Asia. On the sidelines of a Milken Institute Global Conference in Los Angeles earlier this week, Bessent told a crowd that he has been particularly impressed with Indonesia's trade framework, without providing further details, a person in attendance said.

Vice President JD Vance on Wednesday said the U.S. is engaging with "most nations," both friends and adversaries. "We just want a little bit more fairness, or to use the president's word, reciprocity," he said at a conference in Washington, calling trade discussions the "early innings."

Bessent and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are traveling to Switzerland on Thursday to meet Beijing's lead economic representative, the first direct talks over trade between senior officials on both sides.

  • Max Colchester contributed to this article.

Write to Natalie Andrews at natalie.andrews@wsj.com, Brian Schwartz at brian.schwartz@wsj.com and Alex Leary at alex.leary@wsj.com

data.title: Swisscom's first-quarter revenue up 40% after Vodafone Italia acquisition
data.description:
Swisscom reported a 39.3% rise in its first-quarter revenue on Thursday, meeting market expectations, citing the successful acquisition of Vodafone Italia.

Group revenue on a reported basis came in at 3.76 billion Swiss francs ($4.56 billion) in the quarter, in line with analysts' forecast of 3.76 billion francs in a company-provided consensus.

On a pro forma basis, first quarter group revenue fell by 1.2% year-on-year, dragged down by weaker performance on domestic market.

At Swisscom's Italian business revenue for the quarter remained almost stable at 1.82 billion euros, in line with analysts' estimates of 1.81 billion euros.

Today's Swisscom results mark the first quarter since the integration of Vodafone Italia.

In January, the company completed its acquisition of competitor Vodafone Italia, which was consolidated with Swisscom's Italian unit Fastweb.

With slowing growth and intensified competition in the Swiss telecommunication sector, namely Sunrise Communications's intention to grow exclusively on Swiss market, the acquisition should help Swisscom to expand its market share.

"Business performance in the first quarter of 2025 met our expectations. Swisscom is on track," Chief Executive Christoph Aeschlimann said in a statement.

"I am especially pleased that the integration of Vodafone Italia is going as planned," Aeschlimann added.

($1 = 0.8249 Swiss francs)

data.title: Sunstone Metals Identifies Additional Gold-Silver Mineralization at Ecuador Project
data.description:
Sunstone Metals said assays from recent geological mapping and rock chip sampling at its Bramaderos project in Ecuador identified additional areas of epithermal gold-silver mineralization, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The assays included up to 14.4 grams-per-tonne grade of gold from surface rock chip samples, the filing said.

Its managing director, Patrick Duffy, said the results are consistent with its geological model, giving the firm confidence that it can grow the areas of higher-grade gold and deliver multiple drill targets.

Norwegian Air Shuttle Says Booking Trends Support Encouraging Outlook
data.description:
(05:02 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle 1Q Rev NOK6.58B
(05:02 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Analysts Saw 1Q Revenue At NOK6.63B
(05:02 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle 1Q EBIT Loss NOK610.9M
(05:02 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Analysts Saw 1Q Ebit Loss At NOK709M
(05:03 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle 1Q Net Loss NOK756.5M
(05:03 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Analysts Saw 1Q Net Loss At NOK776M
(05:04 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Booking Trends, Improved Supply-and-Demand Balance in Europe Support Encouraging Outlook
(05:05 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Unit Costs Seen Impacted by Reduced Growth in Production, Industry-Specific Inflation
(05:06 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Unit Cost Excluding Fuel in 2025 Seen increasing Mid-Single-Digit Percentage Vs 2024
(05:07 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: 2025 Production Growth Seen at 3%, From 13% in 2024
(05:08 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Reduction in Production Growth Due to Aircraft Delivery Delays From Boeing
(05:10 GMT) *Norwegian Air Shuttle: Current Booking Trend For Summer is Robust, More Tickets Sold Than at Same Period Last Yr
(05:40 GMT) Norwegian Air Shuttle Says Booking Trends Support Encouraging Outlook

By Dominic Chopping

Norwegian Air Shuttle said current booking trends and a tighter supply-and-demand balance in Europe support an encouraging outlook for the airline this year.

The low-cost carrier reported a first-quarter net loss of 756.5 million Norwegian kroner ($73.1 million) compared with a loss of 903.5 million kroner a year earlier.

Total revenue rose 7.1% to 6.58 billion kroner as the group carried 5.06 million passengers compared with 4.75 million a year earlier.

A FactSet poll had expected a net loss of 776 million kroner on revenue of 6.63 billion kroner.

Norwegian said that for 2025 it is now targeting a 3% rise in passenger carrying capacity, having previously seen 4% and compared with 13% in the previous year. The lower growth is mainly due to aircraft delivery delays from Boeing.

Costs this year are expected to be affected by the lower growth in production and industry-specific inflationary pressure. Excluding fuel, 2025 unit costs are expected to increase by a mid-single-digit percentage versus 2024.

"Current booking trend for the summer season is robust and encouraging with more tickets sold this year than at the same period last year," it said.

Norwegian said it is progressing well with profitability initiatives that should deliver recurring underlying profit improvement of over 1 billion kroner by the end of 2026.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: CLSA sees declining crude prices boosting MRF's margins
data.description:
** Shares of tyre maker MRF climb 0.5% to 141,110 rupees

** CLSA raises PT on India's priciest stock to 168,426 rupees from 128,599 rupees; retains "outperform" rating

** CLSA's new PT is the highest among brokerages tracking the stock, compared to the previous Street high of 160,000 rupees by Anand Rathi

** Brokerage says it expects MRF's margins to improve significantly on lower crude oil-based raw material costs and easing Indian rubber prices

** Brent crude futures have dropped 18% so far this year

** CLSA estimates EBITDA margin will improve to 17.3%-17.8% by FY27 from 14.3% in FY25

** Adds, MRF's better portfolio has helped it outperform peers and should also help it generate free cash flow of 27 billion rupees ($318.7 million) in FY27

** Avg rating on the stock is a "hold", compared to "buy" for CEAT , Apollo Tyres and JK Tyre - data compiled by LSEG

** MRF stock's 7.8% YTD climb this year trails only CEAT's 16% rise

($1 = 84.7200 Indian rupees)
